executive_summary: The parallax CLI implementation fully satisfies all 8 user stories and all 9 functional requirements of the PRD. All 37 automated tests pass (0 failures), TypeScript typecheck is clean, and the shebang + bin field are correctly configured. Model validation, required-flag validation, stub execution, and help output at every command level all behave as specified.
minor_observations: FR-9 allows optional video/audio placeholder request/response interfaces in @parallax/sdk — they were not added, which is acceptable per the 'may be added' language in the PRD.

The required-flag error reformatting in configureOutput uses a regex against commander's internal error string. This is slightly brittle if commander changes its error format in a future major version; consider pinning commander to ^12 or testing the exact error message format.

US-008 acceptance criteria AC01 ('bun link succeeds') and AC02 ('parallax --help works from any directory after linking') are configuration-verified but not covered by automated tests. The package.json bin field and shebang are correct, making manual verification straightforward.
conclusions_and_recommendations: The implementation is production-quality for a CLI scaffold iteration. All PRD requirements are met with full test coverage. No remediation is required. Recommended next steps: (1) pin commander version explicitly (e.g. '12.x') to protect the regex-based error reformatter from upstream changes; (2) add a bun-link smoke test to CI if US-008 becomes a regression risk; (3) when model implementations are added in future iterations, replace the stub action with actual pipeline calls and expand the MODELS registry accordingly.
